steps.title: Calculate the y-intercept
steps.content:
The $y$-intercept occurs when $x = 0$. Substitute into the equation:
$$y = 400(3)^{2(0)} = 400(3)^0 = 400 \cdot 1 = 400$$
The $y$-intercept is $(0, 400)$.

steps.title: Interpret in context and match to the choice
steps.content:
The problem states that $x$ represents six-month periods starting from the end of January 1997.

Therefore, $x = 0$ is the starting reference point—the end of January 1997.

The $y$-intercept $(0, 400)$ represents 400 subscribers at the end of January 1997.

Choice A refers to the first six-month period ($x = 1$), which is a different time.

Choice B states exactly what we found: 400 subscribers at the end of January 1997.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
